name: Apache - Identify High Volume of Server Errors
xql_query:
datamodel dataset = apache_httpd_raw
// 1. Explicitly select all relevant XDM fields for SOAR mapping (as performed in other rules)
| fields _time, _product, _vendor, 
xdm.source.ipv4, xdm.source.ipv6, xdm.source.user.username, xdm.source.user_agent, xdm.source.process.pid, xdm.source.process.thread_id,
xdm.source.host.hostname, xdm.target.ipv4, xdm.target.port, xdm.target.domain,
xdm.network.http.method, xdm.network.http.url, xdm.network.http.referrer, xdm.network.http.response_code,
xdm.target.sent_bytes, xdm.network.tls.protocol_version, xdm.event.log_level, xdm.event.description, xdm.observer.name,
xdm.target.file.path, xdm.target.file.filename
// 2. Filter for 5xx Server Errors
| filter to_number(xdm.network.http.response_code) >= 500
// 3. Ensure the grouping field (URL) is not null
| filter xdm.network.http.url != null
// 4. Aggregate the counts by URL, ensuring key XDM fields are passed via min()
| comp count(_time) as server_error_count,
values(xdm.network.http.response_code) as response_codes,
values(xdm.source.ipv4) as source_ips,
values(xdm.source.user_agent) as user_agents_used,
min(_time) as first_occurrence,
max(_time) as last_occurrence,
// --- Contextual XDM Fields carried forward using min() ---
min(xdm.target.ipv4) as server_ip,
min(xdm.target.port) as server_port,
min(xdm.target.domain) as target_domain,
min(xdm.network.http.method) as method
by xdm.network.http.url
// 5. Set the threshold for alerting (e.g., 50 errors on one URL)
| filter server_error_count >= 50
// 6. Select the aggregated results and grouping fields, retaining full context
| fields *
alert_name: Apache - Identify High Volume of Server Errors
